# Jira & Confluence (Atlassian) Scorecard

**Review period:** FY2024  
**Owner:** Finance / Engineering Tooling  
**Classification:** Confidential

## Contract Summary

- **Products:** Jira Software Cloud (Premium), Confluence Cloud (Premium), Jira Service Management (Standard)
- **User tier:** 1,001–2,000 users (flat-rate pricing)
- **Annual spend:** $198,000
- **Contract expiry:** 2025-05-01

## Scorecard

| Dimension | Score (1–5) |
|-----------|-------------|
| Adoption / stickiness | 4.6 |
| Pricing transparency | 2.7 |
| Product velocity | 3.8 |
| Support quality | 3.3 |
| Integration depth | 4.2 |
| **Composite** | **3.72** |

## Pricing Concerns

Atlassian's flat-rate tier jumps from $198K at 2,000 users to $332K at 2,001–3,000 users — a 68% increase for one additional user. We're currently at 1,847 active users and growing at ~15/month. At that pace, we cross 2,000 in approximately 10 months.

**Finance flag:** Plan headcount carefully to stay below 2,000 through next renewal or budget for the tier jump in FY2026.

## Jira Service Management Notes

JSM Standard is used by IT Ops for internal helpdesk. Volume is low (~900 tickets/month). At Standard tier pricing with 12 agents, cost is $24,000/yr. Considering downgrade to Free tier (up to 3 agents) and routing some IT tickets through Zendesk, which we already pay for.

## Atlassian Intelligence (AI Add-On)

Atlassian is bundling AI features into Premium plans without additional charge for now. This may change. Monitor release notes — if they move AI to a paid add-on, it changes the Premium vs Standard calculus.

## Action Items

- [ ] Finance: model tier-jump impact in FY2026 headcount scenarios
- [ ] IT Ops: evaluate JSM Free tier feasibility by 2025-01-30
- [ ] Engineering Tools: audit Confluence spaces for inactive content (storage cost driver)
- [ ] Procurement: request Atlassian renewal quote 120 days early (by 2025-01-01)

Related: `zendesk-vendor-review.md` | `github-enterprise-spend.md`
